revision.comment: 新しいページ: ''''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]...'
revision.text:
'''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。[[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]]八百万神を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。[[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は約4万人。
[[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]]（現在の[[磐田市]]）に[[遷座]]した。
==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（1972年7月から）
==外部リンク== *[http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~kyoutyou/ 神道修成派] *[http://www.keisin.or.jp/ 神道修成派敬神大教会] *[http://oinarisan.ftw.jp/ 萩原大教会]
{{shinto-stub}} [[category:日本の宗教団体|しんとうしゆうせいは]] [[Category:神道の教派|しんとうしゆうせいは]]

[[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]]（現在の[[磐田市]]）に[[遷座]]した。
==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（1972年7月から）

{{戦前までに日本で発祥した新宗教}} {{新宗教}} [[Category:神道の教派|しんとうしゆうせいは]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しんとうしゆうせいは}}

{{神道}} '''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。
[[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]]八百万神を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。
[[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は約4万人。
== 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と１種類の附録で示されている。 === 教 典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附 録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註)
== 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」といいます。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれています。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子が鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にあります。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にあります。南側に[[磐田市立]][[豊岡東小学校]]があります。 教務本部である教庁は、東京都杉並区松庵の【本郷祠】の中にあります。
==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。
[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。
[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。
==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から）
== 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行しております。 ** 月次祭(毎月第１日曜日) ** 星祭大祈祷(２月５日) ** 大祭(４月の第１週の金、土曜日）
* 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行しております。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日)
* 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行しております。 ** 大祭(10月 体育の日）

== 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」といいます。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれています。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にあります。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にあります。南東側に[[磐田市立]][[豊岡東小学校]]があります。 教務本部である教庁は、東京都杉並区松庵の【本郷祠】の中にあります。
==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。
[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。
[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。

* 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行しております。 ** 大祭(10月 体育の日）

{{戦前までに日本で発祥した新宗教}} {{新宗教}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんとうしゆうせいは}} [[Category:神道の教派]] [[Category:御嶽信仰]]

'''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。
[[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]]八百万神を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。
[[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は約1万1千人。
== 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と1種類の附録で示されている。 === 教典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註)
== 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」という。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれている。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にある。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（静岡県磐田市敷地） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にある。南東側に[[磐田市立豊岡東小学校]]がある。
==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。
[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。
[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。
==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から）
== 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日）
* 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日)
* 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月 体育の日）

== 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と1種類の附録で示されている。 === 教典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註)
== 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」という。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれている。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にある。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（静岡県磐田市敷地） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にある。南東側に[[磐田市立豊岡東小学校]]がある。
==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。
[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。
[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。
==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、[[カール・シュミット]]研究者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から）
== 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日）
* 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日)
* 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月 体育の日）

== 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日）
* 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日)
* 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月 体育の日）

*[https://www.mikuni-daikyoukai.org/ 神道修成派美久仁大教会] *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93%E4%BF%AE%E6%88%90%E6%B4%BE%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%83%E7%A5%A0/@34.8479891,137.8772011,18z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x0:0x8be3552a41eaaa2 神道修成派大元祠【山上祠(さんじょうし)】] - [[Google マップ]] より *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93%E4%BF%AE%E6%88%90%E6%B4%BE%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%83%E7%A5%A0/@34.836242,137.864797,19z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m7!1m4!3m3!1s0x601b1d8dcb1d9b1f:0xf9e41c193a3a56c5!2z6Z2Z5bKh55yM56OQ55Sw5biC5pW35Zyw77yZ77yT77yY!3b1!3m1!1s0x0:0xb1565ff3a3b682be 神道修成派大元祠【山下祠(さんかし)】] - [[Google マップ]] より *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E3%80%92167-0054+%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%9D%89%E4%B8%A6%E5%8C%BA%E6%9D%BE%E5%BA%B5%EF%BC%93%E4%B8%81%E7%9B%AE%EF%BC%91%EF%BC%95%E2%88%92%EF%BC%91%EF%BC%92+%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93%E4%BF%AE%E6%88%90%E6%B4%BE%E6%95%99%E5%BA%81%E6%9C%AC%E9%83%B7%E7%A5%A0/@35.7016166,139.5939167,19z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m2!3m1!1s0x6018ee16d5ff2237:0x6096cf9c5b5a8d01 神道修成派教庁【本郷祠】] - [[Google マップ]] より {{神道 横}} {{戦前までに日本で発祥した新宗教}} {{新宗教}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんとうしゆうせいは}} [[Category:神道の教派]] [[Category:御嶽信仰]]

'''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。
[[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]]八百万神を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。
[[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は8,084人<ref>文化庁『宗教年鑑 令和2年版』 P57</ref>。
== 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と1種類の附録で示されている。 === 教典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註)
== 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」という。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれている。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にある。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（静岡県磐田市敷地） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にある。南東側に[[磐田市立豊岡東小学校]]がある。
==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。
[[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。
[[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。
==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、[[カール・シュミット]]研究者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から）
== 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日）
* 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日)
* 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月 体育の日）

{{Infobox 組織 | 名称 =神道修成派 | 画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=杉並区|cat2=磐田市|cat3=宗教}} | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 画像2 = | 画像サイズ2 = | 画像説明2 = | 地図 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=教庁|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | 地図2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=本部教会（大元祠）|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=山上祠|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=山下祠|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | 略称 = | 設立 = 1848年（嘉永元年） | 解散 = | 種類 = [[宗教法人]] | 地位 = | 目的 = | 本部 = [[東京都]][[杉並区]]松庵3-15-12 |coords = 教庁：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E|display=inline,title}}
山上祠：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E|display=inline}}
山下祠：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E|display=inline}} | 位置 = | 言語 = | 設立者 =[[新田邦光]] | 人物 = | 関連組織 = | 機関 = |leader_title=教主|leader_name=[[新田邦夫]] |website= }} '''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。 [[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]][[八百万神]]を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。 [[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は7,004人文化庁『宗教年鑑　令和4年版』 P57。 == 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と1種類の附録で示されている。 === 教典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) == 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」という。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれている。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]1464-1） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にある。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（静岡県磐田市敷地938-17） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にある。南東側に[[磐田市立豊岡東小学校]]がある。 ==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。 [[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。 [[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。 ==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、[[カール・シュミット]]研究者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から） == 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日） * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日) * 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月　体育の日） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ 神道修成派] - 山下祠：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E|display=inline}} | 位置 = | 言語 = | 設立者 =[[新田邦光]] | 人物 = | 関連組織 = | 機関 = |leader_title=教主|leader_name=[[新田邦夫]] |website= }} '''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。 [[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]][[八百万神]]を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。 [[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は7,004人文化庁『宗教年鑑　令和4年版』 P57。 == 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と1種類の附録で示されている。 === 教典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) == 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」という。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれている。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]1464-1） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にある。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（静岡県磐田市敷地938-17） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にある。南東側に[[磐田市立豊岡東小学校]]がある。 ==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。 [[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。 [[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。 ==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、[[カール・シュミット]]研究者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から） == 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日） * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日) * 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月　体育の日） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ 神道修成派] - revision.text:
{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>

=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.

== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

revision.text:
{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>

=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

{{Infobox 組織 | 名称 =神道修成派 | 画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=杉並区|cat2=磐田市|cat3=宗教}} | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 画像2 = | 画像サイズ2 = | 画像説明2 = | 地図 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=教庁（[[東京都]][[杉並区]]松庵）|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | 地図2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=本部教会 (大元祠)（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]）|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=山上祠|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=山下祠|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | 略称 = | 設立 = 1848年（嘉永元年） | 解散 = | 種類 = [[宗教法人]] | 地位 = | 目的 = | 本部 = 教庁：[[東京都]][[杉並区]]松庵3-15-12
山上祠：[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]1464-1
山下祠：静岡県磐田市敷地938-17 |coords = 教庁：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E|display=inline,title}}
山上祠：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E|display=inline}}
山下祠：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E|display=inline}} | 位置 = | 言語 = | 設立者 =[[新田邦光]] | 人物 = | 関連組織 = | 機関 = |leader_title=教主|leader_name=[[新田邦夫]] |website= }} '''神道修成派'''（しんとうしゅうせいは）は、[[東京都]][[杉並区]][[松庵]]に法人本部（教庁）を置く[[教派神道]]で、[[神道十三派]]の一つ。 [[天之御中主神]]・[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐大神]]、[[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[天津神・国津神|天神地祇]][[八百万神]]を総称した修成大神を[[祭神]]としている。 [[教義]]は[[儒教]]の影響を受けている。信者数は6,502人文化庁『宗教年鑑　令和6年版』 P57。 == 文献 == 教義の基礎は、3種類の教典と1種類の附録で示されている。 === 教典 === * 『修道 溯源(しゅうどう そげん)』(大司徒 [[新田邦達]] 訳註) * 『修成道 教典(しゅうせいどう きょうてん)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) * 『顕幽 略説(けんゆう りゃくせつ)』(文学博士 [[佐々木英夫]] 訳註) === 附録 === * 『修成 教法(しゅうせい きょうほう)』(大司徒 新田邦達 訳註) == 二大聖地 == === 大元祠 === 本部教会を「大元祠(だいげんし)」という。山上祠(さんじょうし)と山下祠(さんかし)に分かれている。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】（[[静岡県]][[磐田市]][[敷地]]1464-1） - 本殿が、[[市営]][[獅子ヶ鼻公園]]の北東に隣接する、[[秩山]]の山頂にある。 * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】（静岡県磐田市敷地938-17） - 教祖教霊殿などが、[[天竜浜名湖鉄道]]の[[敷地駅]]より徒歩15分の小高い丘の上にある。南東側に[[磐田市立豊岡東小学校]]がある。 ==歴史== [[新田邦光]]により[[1869年]]（明治2年）に開教し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に修成講社として設立。 [[1876年]]（明治9年）[[10月23日]]に神道修成派として一派独立。 [[1953年]]（昭和28年）4月に本部教会（大元祠）を[[静岡県]][[磐田郡]][[豊岡村 (静岡県)|豊岡村]][[敷地]]（現在の[[磐田市]][[敷地]]）に[[遷座]]した。 ==歴代管長== #新田邦光 #[[新田邦貞]] #[[新田邦達]] #[[新田邦夫]]（[[政治学]]者、[[カール・シュミット]]研究者、元[[山梨大学]][[教育学部]][[教授]]、1972年7月から） == 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山上祠(さんじょうし)】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 月次祭（毎月第1日曜日） ** 星祭大祈祷（2月5日） ** 大祭（4月の第1週の金、土曜日） * 大元祠(だいげんし)【山下祠(さんかし)】の教霊殿にて、下記の霊祭を斎行している。 ** 月次霊祭(毎月25日) ** 教祖教霊祭(11月24日、11月25日) * 教庁【本郷祠】にて、下記の大祭を斎行している。 ** 大祭(10月　体育の日） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ 神道修成派] - 教派神道連合会 *{{Wayback|url=http://www6.ocn.ne.jp/~kyoutyou/ |title=神道修成派 |date=20040110093312}} *[https://keisin.or.jp/ 神道修成派敬神大教会] *[http://oinarisan.ftw.jp/ 萩原大教会] *[https://www.mikuni-daikyoukai.org/ 神道修成派美久仁大教会] *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93%E4%BF%AE%E6%88%90%E6%B4%BE%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%83%E7%A5%A0/@34.8479891,137.8772011,18z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x0:0x8be3552a41eaaa2 神道修成派大元祠【山上祠(さんじょうし)】] - [[Google マップ]] より *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93%E4%BF%AE%E6%88%90%E6%B4%BE%E5%A4%A7%E5%85%83%E7%A5%A0/@34.836242,137.864797,19z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m7!1m4!3m3!1s0x601b1d8dcb1d9b1f:0xf9e41c193a3a56c5!2z6Z2Z5bKh55yM56OQ55Sw5biC5pW35Zyw77yZ77yT77yY!3b1!3m1!1s0x0:0xb1565ff3a3b682be 神道修成派大元祠【山下祠(さんかし)】] - [[Google マップ]] より *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps/place/%E3%80%92167-0054+%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%9D%89%E4%B8%A6%E5%8C%BA%E6%9D%BE%E5%BA%B5%EF%BC%93%E4%B8%81%E7%9B%AE%EF%BC%91%EF%BC%95%E2%88%92%EF%BC%91%EF%BC%92+%E7%A5%9E%E9%81%93%E4%BF%AE%E6%88%90%E6%B4%BE%E6%95%99%E5%BA%81%E6%9C%AC%E9%83%B7%E7%A5%A0/@35.7016166,139.5939167,19z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m2!3m1!1s0x6018ee16d5ff2237:0x6096cf9c5b5a8d01 神道修成派教庁【本郷祠】] - [[Google マップ]] より {{神道 横}} {{戦前までに日本で発祥した新宗教}} {{新宗教}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんとうしゆうせいは}} [[Category:神道の教派]] [[Category:御嶽信仰]]

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).

== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

revision.text:
{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

revision.text:
{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).

=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

{{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | size = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = [[Category:OSM on jawiki]]{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shintō|ja|教派神道}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami, Tokyo|ja|杉並区}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}, as well as the {{ill|Yaoyorozu-no-Kami|ja|八百万神}}.
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5年版宗教年鑑}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|ja|敷地駅}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|ja|磐田市}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
