revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shintō Shūseiha]][[en:Shinto Shusei]][[ja:神道修成派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590921]] Wikidata name: Shintō shūsei-ha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | area = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shintō]], one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in [[:en:Suginami|Suginami, Tokyo]]. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[:en:Hideo Sasaki|Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Shikiji Station|Shikiji Station]] on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shintō]], one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in [[:en:Suginami|Suginami, Tokyo]]. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[:en:Hideo Sasaki|Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Shikiji Station|Shikiji Station]] on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[:en:Hideo Sasaki|Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Shikiji Station|Shikiji Station]] on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shintō Shūseiha]][[en:Shinto Shusei]][[ja:神道修成派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shintō Shūseiha]][[en:Shinto Shusei]][[ja:神道修成派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shintō Shūseiha]][[en:Shinto Shusei]][[ja:神道修成派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590921]] Wikidata name: Shintō shūsei-ha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox organization | name = Shintō Shūsei-ha | image = {{Image requested|cat=Suginami City|cat2=Iwata City|cat3=Religion}} | area = | caption = | map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|text=Kyōchō (Headquarters), [[Suginami, Tokyo|Suginami]], Tokyo|coord={{coord|35|42|6.1|N|139|35|37.8|E}}}} | map2 = {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|34.842258|N|137.870769|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=13|float=center|width=270|caption=Main Shrine (Ōmoto-shi), [[Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata City]], Shizuoka|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|34|50|56.7|N|137|52|40.1|E}}|label1=Yamajō-shi|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|34|50|10.7|N|137|51|53.1|E}}|label2=Yamashita-shi|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} | abbreviation = | formation = 1848 (Kaei 1) | founder = [[Kunikami Nitta]] | leader_title = Kyōshu (Leader) | leader_name = [[Kunio Nitta]] | headquarters = : Kyōchō: Matsuan, Suginami, Tokyo : Yamajō-shi: Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata, Shizuoka : Yamashita-shi: Shikiji 938-17, Iwata, Shizuoka | website = }}
'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shintō]], one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in [[:en:Suginami|Suginami, Tokyo]]. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[:en:Hideo Sasaki|Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Shikiji Station|Shikiji Station]] on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of [[:en:Sect Shinto|Sect Shintō]], one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in [[:en:Suginami|Suginami, Tokyo]]. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. [[:en:Hideo Sasaki|Hideo Sasaki]]
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Shikiji Station|Shikiji Station]] on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]
{{translated page|ja|神道修成派|version=103334495|comment=Imported full ja history}}

=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shinto|en|Sect Shinto|WD=Q1532150|lt=Sect Shintō}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|WD=Q232631|lt=Suginami, Tokyo}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. {{ill|Hideo Sasaki|en|Hideo Sasaki|WD=Q6654815|lt=Hideo Sasaki}}
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|en|Shikiji Station|WD=Q5370077|lt=Shikiji Station}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shinto|en|Sect Shinto|WD=Q1532150|lt=Sect Shintō}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|WD=Q232631|lt=Suginami, Tokyo}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. {{ill|Hideo Sasaki|en|Hideo Sasaki|WD=Q6654815|lt=Hideo Sasaki}}
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|en|Shikiji Station|WD=Q5370077|lt=Shikiji Station}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

'''Shintō Shūsei-ha''' (神道修成派) is a branch of {{ill|Sect Shinto|en|Sect Shinto|WD=Q1532150|lt=Sect Shintō|1=Sect Shinto}}, one of the {{ill|Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja|神道十三派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thirteen Shintō Sects|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 教派神道|1=Sect Shinto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1532150}}, headquartered in {{ill|Suginami|en|Suginami|WD=Q232631|lt=Suginami, Tokyo|1=Suginami}}. Its primary deities, collectively called Shūsei Ōkami, include {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami-Musubi|ja|高皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|Kami-Musubi|ja|神皇産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, and {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, as well as the [[Yaoyorozu no Kami]].
The sect incorporates elements of Confucianism in its teachings and has a membership of 6,702 adherents as of the {{ill|2023 Religious Statistics|ja|令和5th year 版宗教年鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2023 Religious Statistics|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Religious Yearbook 2023," p.57.</ref>
== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. {{ill|Hideo Sasaki|en|Hideo Sasaki|WD=Q6654815|lt=Hideo Sasaki|1=Hideo Sasaki}}
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|en|Shikiji Station|WD=Q5370077|lt=Shikiji Station|1=Shikiji Station}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
== External Links == * [https://kyoharen.jp/加盟１２教派/shintouha/ Shintō Shūsei-ha - Federation of Sect Shintō Associations] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=35.7016166,139.5939167 Headquarters on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.8479891,137.8772011 Yamajō-shi on Google Maps] * [https://www.google.com/maps?q=34.836242,137.864797 Yamashita-shi on Google Maps]

== Sacred Texts == The doctrinal foundation of Shintō Shūsei-ha is found in three main texts and one supplement: === Main Texts === * ''Shūdō Sogen'' (修道 溯源) – annotated by Taishi [[Kunimichi Nitta]] * ''Shūseidō Kyōten'' (修成道 教典) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta * ''Ken'yū Ryakusetu'' (顕幽 略説) – annotated by Dr. {{ill|Hideo Sasaki|en|Hideo Sasaki|WD=Q6654815|lt=Hideo Sasaki|1=Hideo Sasaki}}
=== Supplement === * ''Shūsei Kyōhō'' (修成 教法) – annotated by Taishi Kunimichi Nitta
== Major Sacred Sites == === Ōmoto-shi === The main shrine is known as "Ōmoto-shi" (大元祠) and consists of two locations: * '''Yamajō-shi''' (山上祠): Located at Shikiji 1464-1, Iwata City, this site includes the main sanctuary on the summit of {{ill|Chiyama|ja|秩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Yamashita-shi''' (山下祠): Situated at Shikiji 938-17, Iwata City, this site features the Founder’s Memorial Hall. It is a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Shikiji Station|en|Shikiji Station|WD=Q5370077|lt=Shikiji Station|1=Shikiji Station}} on the Tenryū Hamanako Line.
== History == Shintō Shūsei-ha was established by [[Kunikami Nitta]] in 1869 (Meiji 2). It became independent as a recognized sect on October 23, 1876 (Meiji 9). The headquarters were moved to their current location in {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in 1953 (Shōwa 28).
== Leadership == 1. Kunikami Nitta 2. Kunisada Nitta 3. Kunimichi Nitta 4. Kunio Nitta (political scientist and former professor at Yamanashi University, appointed in July 1972)
== Annual Rituals == The following are the principal rituals observed: === At Yamajō-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Festivals (First Sunday of each month) * Star Festival Grand Prayer (February 5) * Grand Festival (First weekend of April)
=== At Yamashita-shi (Ōmoto-shi) === * Monthly Spirit Festival (25th of each month) * Founder’s Spirit Festival (November 24-25)
=== At Kyōchō (Headquarters) === * Grand Festival (October, Sports Day)
